name: Temple Mount
shortDescription: The Temple Mount is a highly revered site in Judaism, considered the holiest place in the world for Jews. It is the location of the First and Second Temples and holds immense religious and historical significance.
description: The Temple Mount, known in Hebrew as Har HaBayit (הַר הַבַּיִת), is a hill in the Old City of Jerusalem that has been venerated for thousands of years and is a site of major religious significance in Judaism, Islam, and Christianity. In Jewish tradition, it is the location of the First Temple, built by King Solomon in the 10th century BCE, which housed the Ark of the Covenant and served as the primary center of Jewish worship. The First Temple was destroyed by the Babylonians in 586 BCE. The Second Temple was built in its place in the late 6th century BCE and was later expanded by Herod the Great. This Second Temple stood until its destruction by the Romans in 70 CE. The Western Wall, or Kotel, is a retaining wall of the Temple Mount and a remnant of the Second Temple complex, serving as a focal point for Jewish prayer and pilgrimage. The Temple Mount is believed to be the location where Abraham prepared to sacrifice his son Isaac (Genesis 22), and where God gathered the dust to create Adam. Jewish tradition anticipates the rebuilding of a Third Temple on the Temple Mount in the future Messianic era, symbolizing the restoration of God's presence and the renewal of the covenant with Israel. The Shekhinah, the divine presence, is said to have dwelt in the Temple. The site is also associated with prophecies about the end of days and the ultimate redemption of humanity. The absence of the Temple is mourned during the annual fast of Tisha B'Av.

location.cosmological: Located in the Old City of Jerusalem; earthly location with profound spiritual and cosmological significance as the connection point between heaven and earth in Jewish tradition.
